title: Arthur the King Box Office Performance - Mark Wahlberg's Top 3 Debut
text: Mark Wahlberg's Arthur the King debuted at the domestic box office with a projected opening weekend total of approximately $8 million, placing it at number 3 for the weekend. The film trailed behind two established releases: Dune: Part Two at number 2 and Kung Fu Panda 4 at number 1. However, it outperformed every other title that debuted the same weekend, including The American Society of Magical Negroes, Snack Shack, One Life, and Knox Goes Away. Despite the modest opening, the movie ranks as the 27th highest-grossing film domestically for 2024. When compared specifically to other dramas released in 2024, Arthur the King ranks fourth, behind Bob Marley: One Love ($91 million), Ordinary Angels ($17 million), and Cabrini ($10.1 million). The film is based on a true story about mountain climbers befriending a stray dog and was designed as a smaller-scale drama that wasn't necessarily designed to compete with a science fiction epic or major animated franchises.
